prompts.text: {player1}, dive in! Take 2 sips to start the expedition.
{player1}, give out 3 sips -- you're the captain of this ship.
{player1}, take 3 sips -- the ocean is deep and so is your glass.
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever is most afraid of the ocean.
{player1}, take 2 sips. We're going below the surface.
{player1}, hand out 2 sips to whoever couldn't survive on a desert island.
First to shout it out: How many bones does a shark have? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Zero -- sharks have no bones. Their skeleton is made entirely of cartilage.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the largest species of shark (and the largest fish in the world)? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The whale shark -- up to 40 feet long. Despite its size, it's a filter feeder that eats plankton.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many rows of teeth can a great white shark have? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Up to 5 rows at a time -- they can go through 20,000+ teeth in a lifetime because new ones constantly replace old ones

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What shark can survive in both salt water and fresh water? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The bull shark -- they've been found in rivers, lakes, and even golf course ponds hundreds of miles from the ocean

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the fastest shark in the ocean? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The shortfin mako shark -- up to 46 mph, making it faster than most boats

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What shark glows in the dark? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The kitefin shark -- at up to 6 feet long, it's the largest known bioluminescent vertebrate on Earth

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How old can Greenland sharks live to be? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Over 400 years -- they're the longest-lived vertebrates known. Some alive today were born before Shakespeare.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a group of sharks called? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A shiver (or a school/frenzy)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What prehistoric shark was the largest predatory fish ever and had teeth the size of a human hand? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Megalodon -- estimated 50-60 feet long. It went extinct about 3.6 million years ago.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the largest animal to have EVER lived on Earth, including dinosaurs? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The blue whale -- up to 100 feet long and 200 tons. Larger than any dinosaur that ever existed.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How loud is a blue whale's call? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Up to 188 decibels -- louder than a jet engine (140 dB). Their calls can be heard by other whales over 1,000 miles away.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: A blue whale's heart is the size of what? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A small car (a VW Beetle) -- it weighs about 400 pounds and a child could crawl through its arteries

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What type of whale is known for its complex songs that can last for hours? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The humpback whale -- males sing elaborate songs during mating season. All males in a population sing the same song, and it evolves over time.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Are dolphins technically whales? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Yes -- dolphins are part of the order Cetacea and the family Delphinidae. Orcas (killer whales) are actually the largest dolphins.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most dangerous whale to other marine animals? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The orca (killer whale) -- they hunt great whites, other whales, seals, and even moose swimming between islands

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What do orcas do to great white sharks that terrifies the sharks into fleeing an area for months? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: They flip the sharks upside down to induce tonic immobility (a trance state), then surgically remove and eat just the liver -- the fattiest, most nutrient-rich organ

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How long can a sperm whale hold its breath? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Up to 90 minutes -- they can dive over 7,000 feet deep to hunt giant squid

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What substance found in sperm whale intestines was once worth more than gold? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: Ambergris -- a waxy substance produced in their digestive system. It was used in perfume and could wash up on beaches worth tens of thousands of dollars per pound.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Narwhals have a long tusk. What is it actually? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A tooth -- specifically, an elongated upper left canine that can grow up to 10 feet long. It has millions of nerve endings and may sense changes in water.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Do dolphins sleep? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Yes, but only half their brain at a time -- it's called unihemispheric sleep. One eye stays open. They'd drown if they fully lost consciousness.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many hearts does an octopus have? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: 3 -- two pump blood to the gills, one pumps it to the body. Their blood is blue because it uses copper-based hemocyanin instead of iron.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What color is octopus blood? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Blue -- it uses copper-based hemocyanin to carry oxygen, which is more efficient in cold, low-oxygen deep water

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many brains does an octopus have? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Technically 9 -- one central brain and a cluster of neurons in each of its 8 arms that can act independently

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What happens to a male octopus after mating? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: He dies -- within weeks or months of mating. Females also die shortly after their eggs hatch. They stop eating to guard their eggs.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How big can a giant Pacific octopus get? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Up to 16 feet across (arm span) and over 100 pounds -- the largest octopus species

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What deep-sea creature has the largest eyes in the animal kingdom? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The colossal squid -- its eyes can be up to 11 inches in diameter, roughly the size of a dinner plate

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Can octopuses taste with their arms? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Yes -- they have chemoreceptors (taste sensors) in their suckers. They literally taste everything they touch.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the only invertebrate proven to use tools? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The veined octopus -- it carries coconut shells to use as portable shelter. They're remarkably intelligent.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How fast can a mimic octopus change its appearance? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: In milliseconds -- the mimic octopus can impersonate over 15 different species including lionfish, flatfish, and sea snakes

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most venomous marine animal in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The box jellyfish (Chironex fleckeri) -- its venom can kill a human in minutes. It's found mainly off the coast of Australia.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Is a jellyfish a fish? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: No -- jellyfish aren't fish at all. They have no brain, no heart, no blood, and no bones. They're 95% water.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What jellyfish is considered biologically immortal? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Turritopsis dohrnii -- the 'immortal jellyfish.' When stressed or aging, it can revert to its juvenile polyp stage and start its life cycle over.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a group of jellyfish called? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A smack (or a bloom/swarm)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The Portuguese man-of-war looks like a jellyfish but isn't one. What is it? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A siphonophore -- it's actually a colony of specialized organisms working together as one. Each 'part' is a different organism.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What percentage of the ocean has been explored by humans? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: About 5% -- we know more about the surface of Mars than our own ocean floor

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the deepest point in the ocean? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The Challenger Deep in the Mariana Trench -- about 36,000 feet (11,000 meters) deep. That's deeper than Mount Everest is tall.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What deep-sea fish has a light dangling from its head to lure prey? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The anglerfish -- only the females have the bioluminescent lure. Males are tiny and permanently fuse to the female's body.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What happens to an anglerfish male when it finds a female? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: It bites into her skin, fuses to her body, and gradually loses its eyes, organs, and brain -- becoming nothing but a pair of gonads that she uses to fertilize eggs. Multiple males can fuse to one female.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is bioluminescence? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The ability of living organisms to produce light through chemical reactions -- about 76% of deep-sea creatures are bioluminescent

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What lives near hydrothermal vents at the bottom of the ocean where temperatures can reach 750 degrees F? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Giant tube worms, specialized shrimp, and bacteria that use chemosynthesis instead of photosynthesis. These ecosystems exist without any sunlight.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What deep-sea creature looks like it's from an alien movie, has transparent skin, and is called a 'vampire'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The vampire squid (Vampyroteuthis infernalis -- literally 'vampire squid from Hell'). Despite the name, it's harmless and eats marine snow (dead organic matter).

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the 'bloop'? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: An ultra-low-frequency underwater sound detected in 1997 that was one of the loudest sounds ever recorded. It was likely caused by an icequake (a large iceberg cracking).

prompts.text: First to shout it out: At what depth does sunlight stop reaching in the ocean? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 3,280 feet (1,000 meters) -- below this is the 'midnight zone' of permanent darkness

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Is coral an animal, plant, or mineral? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: An animal -- corals are colonies of tiny animals called polyps. They have a symbiotic relationship with algae (zooxanthellae) that gives them color.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is coral bleaching? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: When stressed corals expel their symbiotic algae, turning white. It's primarily caused by warming ocean temperatures. If conditions don't improve, the coral dies.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What percentage of marine species depend on coral reefs? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 25% -- despite covering less than 1% of the ocean floor, coral reefs support a quarter of all marine life

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How long can sea turtles live? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Some species can live over 100 years -- the oldest known sea turtle was estimated at 150+ years

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How do sea turtles find the beach where they were born to lay their own eggs? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: They use Earth's magnetic field -- each beach has a unique magnetic signature that turtles imprint on as hatchlings and remember for decades

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Why do baby sea turtles rush to the ocean at night? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: They navigate by moonlight reflecting off the water. Artificial lights from cities can confuse them, causing them to go the wrong direction.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What determines the sex of sea turtle eggs? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Temperature -- warmer sand produces more females, cooler sand produces more males. Climate change is causing up to 99% female hatchlings in some populations.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What sea creature has a punch so powerful it can break aquarium glass? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The mantis shrimp -- its strike accelerates at 10,400 g-force (faster than a .22 caliber bullet) and generates heat almost as hot as the surface of the sun

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What fish can change from female to male (or male to female)? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Clownfish -- they live in groups where the dominant fish is female. If she dies, the largest male becomes female. Finding Nemo would've been a very different movie.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What marine animal has survived all five mass extinctions? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Horseshoe crabs -- they've been around for over 450 million years, older than dinosaurs. Their blue blood is used in medical testing.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What color is horseshoe crab blood and why is it valuable? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Blue -- it contains a substance (LAL) that detects bacterial contamination. A quart of horseshoe crab blood is worth about $15,000. Every vaccine and injectable drug is tested with it.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What sea creature can regenerate its entire body from just a small piece? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Starfish (sea stars) -- some species can regrow from a single arm as long as part of the central disc is attached

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the world's most venomous fish? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The stonefish -- it looks exactly like a rock on the ocean floor. Stepping on one causes excruciating pain so severe that victims have begged for amputation.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What sea creature shoots its own stomach out of its body to eat? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The starfish -- it pushes its stomach out through its mouth, wraps it around prey, digests it externally, then pulls the stomach back in

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What fish can walk on land? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The mudskipper -- it uses its fins to 'walk' and can breathe through its skin. Also, the climbing perch and some catfish species.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the loudest animal in the ocean? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The sperm whale -- its clicks can reach 230 decibels, loud enough to vibrate your internal organs if you were nearby. The pistol shrimp's snap is also incredibly loud.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What tiny shrimp creates a sound so loud it can stun prey? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The pistol shrimp (snapping shrimp) -- it snaps its claw so fast it creates a cavitation bubble that reaches 4,700 degrees Celsius and produces a shockwave of 218 decibels

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What marine creature is essentially a living stomach with no brain? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The sea sponge -- it has no nervous system, no brain, no organs. It just filters water through its body.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the fastest fish in the ocean? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The sailfish -- it can swim up to 68 mph (110 km/h)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What sea creature has teeth on its tongue? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The limpet -- its tongue (radula) has teeth made of the strongest biological material known, stronger than spider silk and even Kevlar

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What tiny organism produces over 50% of the world's oxygen? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Phytoplankton (marine algae) -- they produce more oxygen than all the world's forests combined

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the smallest seahorse in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Hippocampus denise -- about the size of a grain of rice (less than half an inch). It was only discovered in 2003.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In seahorses, who carries the babies? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The male -- he has a brood pouch where the female deposits eggs. He fertilizes and carries them until birth. Males literally give birth.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What ocean creature has been found alive at the bottom of the Mariana Trench? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Amphipods (tiny crustaceans), xenophyophores (giant single-celled organisms), and shrimp-like creatures. Life exists at the deepest point on Earth.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a 'dead zone' in the ocean? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: An area with so little oxygen that most marine life can't survive. There are over 400 dead zones worldwide, mostly caused by fertilizer runoff creating algae blooms.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What marine animal sleeps with one eye open, similar to dolphins? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Crocodiles and some species of seals -- they use unihemispheric sleep, keeping one brain hemisphere active

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the Great Pacific Garbage Patch? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: A massive concentration of floating plastic debris in the North Pacific Ocean -- it's estimated to be twice the size of Texas

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What color is a lobster's blood? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Clear/slightly blue -- like octopuses, lobsters use copper-based hemocyanin. Their blood turns blue when exposed to oxygen.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many species are estimated to live in the ocean? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: An estimated 700,000+ known species, but scientists believe 2-3 million species remain undiscovered

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What ocean creature can survive being frozen, boiled, exposed to radiation, and the vacuum of space? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The tardigrade (water bear) -- microscopic creatures found in ocean sediment. They're virtually indestructible.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What whale exploded on a street in Taiwan in 2004? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: A sperm whale -- gases built up inside the decomposing carcass as it was being transported through the city, and it exploded, showering bystanders and buildings with whale guts

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What fish can inflate itself to several times its normal size? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The pufferfish (blowfish) -- it fills with water or air. Many species are also extremely poisonous (tetrodotoxin), yet fugu is a delicacy in Japan.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What marine creature has the most legs? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Some species of sea centipedes have over 750 legs, but for 'true' marine animals, the basket star (a type of brittle star) has highly branched arms that look like thousands of tiny legs

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What ocean animal has a brain that wraps around its throat? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: The octopus -- its brain is donut-shaped and wraps around its esophagus. If it eats something too big, it can get brain damage.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the saltiest body of water on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Don Juan Pond in Antarctica -- it's 44% salt (about 12 times saltier than the ocean). The Dead Sea is second.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What percentage of Earth's surface is covered by ocean? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: About 71%

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How deep would the ocean need to be to submerge Mount Everest? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Mariana Trench is already deeper than Everest is tall. If you put Everest at the bottom, there would still be over a mile of water above it.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What ocean is the warmest? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The Indian Ocean

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How much of the Earth's total water is in the oceans? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 97% -- only 3% is freshwater, and most of that is locked in ice caps

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What creates ocean tides? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The gravitational pull of the Moon (and to a lesser extent, the Sun)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What ocean current keeps Europe warmer than it should be for its latitude? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Gulf Stream (part of the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation) -- without it, London would be as cold as Labrador, Canada

Everyone must make a fish face before drinking for {rule_turns} rounds. No fish face = extra sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: Fish faces are done. You can drink like a human again.

prompts.text: No saying any sea creature names for {rule_turns} rounds. Slip up = 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Sea creature names are allowed again.

prompts.text: {player1} is the Captain. Everyone must get their approval before drinking for {rule_turns} rounds.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1} has walked the plank. No more captain.

prompts.text: For {rule_turns} rounds, you must say 'glub glub' before drinking. Skip it = extra sip.
Everyone must speak like a pirate for {rule_turns} rounds. Normal voice = 2 sips.
No one can say 'water' or 'ocean' for {rule_turns} rounds. Slip up = 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: You can say water and ocean again.

prompts.text: {player1} is Thumbmaster. When they put their thumb on the table, last to notice takes 2 sips. {rule_turns} rounds.
No pointing for {rule_turns} rounds. If you point, take 2 sips.
For {rule_turns} rounds, drink with your non-dominant hand. Wrong hand = 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Drink with whatever hand you want.

prompts.text: {player1} picks a word. For {rule_turns} rounds, whoever says that word takes 2 sips.
For {rule_turns} rounds, anyone who laughs takes 1 sip.
Everyone must whisper for {rule_turns} rounds. Full volume = 2 sips.
For {rule_turns} rounds, before drinking you must name a sea creature. Can't? Double sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: No more pre-drink sea creature naming.

prompts.text: For {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must do a wave (the stadium wave) before drinking. Skip it = 1 sip.
For {rule_turns} rounds, you must hold your drink above your head like a snorkel when not sipping. Drop it = 2 sips.
For {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must make a whale sound before answering. No sound = 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: Whale sounds are done. Thank the ocean.

prompts.text: For {rule_turns} rounds, the last person to answer takes an extra sip. Speed matters in the deep.
For {rule_turns} rounds, every time you drink you must say 'Fish are friends, not food.' Skip it = extra sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: You don't have to quote Finding Nemo anymore.

prompts.text: {player1} is the Marine Biologist. They can ask anyone an ocean question for {rule_turns} rounds. Wrong answer = 2 sips.
For {rule_turns} rounds, you must do a swimming motion with your arms after every answer. Skip it = 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: Swimming motions are done. You're on dry land.

{player1}, take {sip_count} sips -- the kraken has spoken.
{player1}, take 4 sips. You've been swallowed by a whale.
{player1}, take 5 sips. Lost at sea.
{player1}, take a shot. Can't? Take 4 sips instead.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. The tide pulled you under.
{player1}, take 4 sips. Stung by a jellyfish.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. Shark attack on your sobriety.

{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. No lifeboat will save you.
{player1}, waterfall your drink for 5 seconds. Tidal wave.
{player1}, take 4 sips. The abyss stares back.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. Pulled into the Mariana Trench.
{player1}, take a shot or finish your drink. Captain's final order.
{player1}, take 5 sips. Davy Jones demands it.

Heaven! Last person to point up drinks 3 sips.
Floor is lava! Last person to point down drinks 3 sips.
{player1}, Would You Rather: swim with great whites for 10 minutes OR drink 5 sips right now? Group votes -- bad choice = 3 sips.
Everyone close your eyes. {player1} picks someone to take 3 secret sips. Open your eyes. Guess who drank!
Ocean fact battle! Go around sharing ocean facts. First to repeat or have no fact takes 4 sips.
Everyone take {sip_count} sips -- the tide is rising.
Everyone vote: scariest ocean creature. Anyone in the minority takes 2 sips.
Breath-holding contest! Everyone holds their breath. Last one standing gives out 3 sips.
Freeze! Last person to freeze in place drinks 3 sips.
{player1}, do your best sea creature impression. Group guesses what it is. Nobody gets it? Take 5 sips.
